title: Client accepts unvalidated persisted UI language
reasoning: The client applies the localStorage value directly as an i18next language even though the rest of the initialization is constrained to supported languages. Because localStorage is user-controlled and can contain stale or arbitrary values, a browser with ui_lang set to an unsupported value can switch the client i18n instance away from the SSR-supported locale after hydration, producing fallback or inconsistent language state in components that branch on i18n.language.
reproduction: In a browser console before loading the app, set localStorage.setItem("ui_lang", "fr") and reload. The client entry calls i18next.changeLanguage("fr") even though only the app-supported languages should be selectable.
recommendation: Validate savedLang against supportedLngs before calling changeLanguage, and optionally remove invalid stored values. Wrap localStorage access in a small try/catch if the app should tolerate storage-denied browsers.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The linked Vitest tests cover renderer, chunking, ingestion, Gemini schemas, Google Forms utilities, and page access helpers, but none exercise wiki/app/entry.client.tsx hydration or persisted language restoration.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a client-entry or extracted helper test that seeds ui_lang with an unsupported value and asserts changeLanguage is not called unless the value is included in supportedLngs.
